title: Fry-grind-rice-khao-khua Kenyan Pork Shoulder Fried Rice
description: Nutty, snappy, and festive Kenyan dish featuring pork shoulder, fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, and pescatarian-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of cooking oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced carrots and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the trimmed green beans and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until they are tender.
Add the pre-cooked and cooled fry-grind-rice-khao-khua and stir to combine with the vegetables.
Add the soy sauce and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes, until the ingredients are well coated.
Add the full-fat coconut milk and stir-fry for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the mixture is heated through.
Add the cooked pork shoulder and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes, until everything is well combined.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped green onions if desired.
